data.examQue:
HOTSPOT -
You are developing the shopping on-the-go project.
You need to build the Adaptive Card for the chatbot.
How should you complete the code? To answer, select the appropriate options in the answer area.
NOTE: Each correct selection is worth one point.
Hot Area:
data.examAnsDesc:
"text": "${name[language]}"

Logic: This expression uses bracket notation to access a property dynamically.

Explanation: If your data object has multiple name translations (e.g., "en": "Apple", "fr": "Pomme") and a variable called language (e.g., set to "en"), the expression ${name[language]} evaluates to name['en'].

Why others are wrong: if(language == 'en'...) is overly complex and limited to one language. name or name.en are hard-coded and won't adapt if the user's language changes.

"$when": "${stockLevel != 'OK'}"

Logic: The $when property determines if a UI element should be rendered based on a boolean condition.

Explanation: The element below this line has color: Attention (usually red). It makes logical sense to show a "Warning/Attention" block only when the stock level is not 'OK'.

Why others are wrong: ${stockLevel == 'OK'} would show the warning only when everything is fine, which is counter-intuitive for an "Attention" color. ${stockLevel.OK} is syntactically incorrect unless stockLevel was an object containing a key named OK.

${image.altText[language]}"

Logic: This combines dot notation (to enter the image object) and bracket notation (to select the language-specific string).

Explanation: image.altText accesses the dictionary of alternate text descriptions. Adding [language] allows the template to pull the description that matches the current language variable (e.g., English, Spanish, etc.).

Why others are wrong: image.altText.language would look for a literal key named "language" rather than using the value of the language variable. image.altText["language"] does the same thing.

data.examQue:
You are developing the chatbot.
You create the following components:
✑ A QnA Maker resource
✑ A chatbot by using the Azure Bot Framework SDK
You need to integrate the components to meet the chatbot requirements.
Which property should you use?
  • A. QnAMakerOptions.StrictFilters
  • B. QnADialogResponseOptions.CardNoMatchText
  • C. QnAMakerOptions.RankerType
  • D. QnAMakerOptions.ScoreThreshold
data.examAnsDesc:
Technical justification

  • Why option D (QnAMakerOptions.ScoreThreshold) is the right choice

    • The ScoreThreshold property defines the minimum confidence score that the QnA Maker service must achieve before it returns an answer.
    • In a bot that combines QnA Maker with the Azure Bot Framework SDK, this threshold is used to decide whether the bot should respond with a QnA answer, fall back to a generic message, or hand off to another dialog.
    • Setting an appropriate score threshold therefore integrates the QnA resource with the bot logic, ensuring that only high‑confidence QnA results are presented to the user and that low‑confidence queries are handled by alternative dialog paths.
    • This directly satisfies the requirement to “integrate the components to meet the chatbot requirements.”

  • Why the other options are less suitable

    • A. QnAMakerOptions.StrictFilters – Enables strict filtering of KB content, but it does not control the confidence level used to trigger a response; it only narrows the search space.
    • B. QnADialogResponseOptions.CardNoMatchText – Specifies the text shown when no card matches a query, which is a UI fallback, not a mechanism for blending QnA results into the overall dialog flow.
    • C. QnAMakerOptions.RankerType – Determines which ranking algorithm (e.g., “activeLearning” or “auto”) is used to order answers; it influences answer ordering but does not set a confidence cut‑off for integration with the bot.

Conclusion – QnAMakerOptions.ScoreThreshold is the only property that explicitly provides the confidence‑based gate needed to merge QnA Maker output with the Bot Framework SDK dialog architecture.

References

  • QnAMakerOptions.ScoreThreshold: https://learn.microsoft.com/azure/cognitive-services/qnamaker/how-to/settings Anat
  • Integrating QnA Maker with Bot Framework: https://learn.microsoft.com/azure/bot-service/how-to/add-qnamaker-to-bot?view=azure-bot-service-4.0
